entry.title: SAP: Creating Excel Files Using cl_cmcb_excel_2007 in AL11 or as an attachment for email
entry.content:
One of the powerful tools available in SAP for generating Excel reports is the cl_cmcb_excel_2007 class. This class provides flexibility and reliability, allowing you to add Excel files to the application server or attach them to emails seamlessly. In this blog post, I'll explain about how to use this class to create a structured Excel sheet and save it to the application server or send it as an email attachment.

Overview

The code snippet below demonstrates how to create an Excel sheet containing outstanding details using the cl_cmcb_excel_2007 class. The process includes setting up the spreadsheet, adding necessary data, and finally saving or emailing the file.

 
DATA(lo_components) = CAST cl_abap_structdescr( cl_abap_typedescr=>describe_by_data( ls_bsid_out ) )->get_components( ).  
  
DATA(lo_excel)  = NEW cl_cmcb_excel_2007( ).  
lo_excel->add_sheet( i_sheetname = 'OS details' ).  
  
" Adding Titles  
lo_excel->set_span_row(  
  i_row_index    = 1  
  i_cellstyle    = lo_excel->c_cellstyle_title  
  i_data         = 'Annexure-1:'  
  i_sheetname    = 'OS details' ).  
lo_excel->set_span_row(  
  i_row_index    = 2  
  i_cellstyle    = lo_excel->c_cellstyle_subtitle  
  i_data         = 'Company XYZ'  
  i_sheetname    = 'OS details' ).  
  
" Adding Headers  
DO 6 TIMES.  
  CASE sy-index.  
    WHEN 1.  
      lv_val = 'Document Date'.  
    WHEN 2.  
      lv_val = 'Invoice No'.  
    WHEN 3.  
      lv_val = 'Customer Code'.  
    WHEN 4.  
      lv_val = 'Customer Name & Location'.  
    WHEN 5.  
      lv_val = 'Type'.  
    WHEN 6.  
      lv_val = 'Balance Due (Amount in Rs.)'.  
  ENDCASE.  
  
  lo_excel->set_column_width(  
    i_width     = 30  
    i_col_index = sy-index  
    i_sheetname = 'OS details').  
  
  lo_excel->set_cell(  
    EXPORTING  
      i_row_index    = 4  
      i_col_index    = sy-index  
      i_cellstyle    = lo_excel->c_cellstyle_header  
      i_data         = lv_val  
      i_sheetname    = 'OS details' ).  
  
  CLEAR lv_val.  
ENDDO.
LOOP AT lt_bsid_out INTO ls_bsid_out.  
  DATA(row_id) = sy-tabix + 4.  
  DATA(col_id) = 1.  
  LOOP AT lo_components INTO DATA(ls_component).  
    ASSIGN COMPONENT ls_component-name  
    OF STRUCTURE ls_bsid_out TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_val>).  
    IF sy-subrc = 0.  
      IF ls_component-name = 'NAME_ORG1'.  
        <lfs_val> = |{ <lfs_val> }_{ ls_email_tmp-ort01 },{ ls_email_tmp-land1 }|.  
      ELSEIF ls_component-name = 'DMBTR'.  
        CONDENSE <lfs_val>.  
      ENDIF.  
  
      lo_excel->set_cell(  
        EXPORTING  
          i_row_index    = row_id  
          i_col_index    = col_id  
          i_cellstyle    = lo_excel->c_cellstyle_normal  
          i_data         = <lfs_val>  
          i_sheetname    = 'OS details' ).  
  
      col_id = col_id + 1.  
    ENDIF.  
    CLEAR : ls_component, <lfs_val>.  
  ENDLOOP.  
  CLEAR: ls_bsid_out, row_id, col_id.  
ENDLOOP.  
" Convert to XSTRING for email attachment  
DATA(lr_xml) = lo_excel->transform( ).  
IF sy-subrc = 0.  
  APPEND VALUE #( attach_type = 'XLS'  
                  attach_subject = 'Annexures'  
                  attach_xstring = lr_xml  
                  ) TO lt_attachments.  
  CLEAR lr_xml.  
ENDIF.  
  
  
" Save the file on the application server  
IF lr_xml IS NOT INITIAL.  
  OPEN DATASET lv_file FOR OUTPUT IN BINARY MODE.  
  IF sy-subrc EQ 0.  
    TRANSFER lr_xml TO lv_file.  
    CLOSE DATASET lv_file.  
    IF sy-subrc = 0.  
      MESSAGE 'File uploaded successfully' TYPE 'I'.  
    ENDIF.  
  ELSE.  
    MESSAGE 'No authorization to create AL11 file' TYPE 'I'.  
  ENDIF.  
ELSE.  
  MESSAGE 'Error while generating Excel file' TYPE 'I'.  
ENDIF. 

FREE: lo_excel, lo_components.

Explanation

  1. Initialization and Setup: We begin by initializing the cl_cmcb_excel_2007 object and adding a new sheet named 'OS details'.

  2. Adding Titles and Headers: The sheet is populated with a title, a subtitle, and several headers for columns. This is done using the set_span_row method. And then we add internal table data using set_cell method, which allow specifying styles and data.

  3. Creating the Excel File: The transform method converts the Excel content into an XSTRING format, which can be used for email attachments or storing as .XLS file in AL11.

  4. Saving or Sending the File: The Excel file can be saved to the application server or sent as an email attachment. The code provides mechanisms for both, using SAP's dataset operations and email classes.

Conclusion

This approach using the cl_cmcb_excel_2007 class is highly adaptable for using Excel features within SAP. By following the steps outlined above, you can efficiently generate, format, and distribute Excel files.

Happy coding!

entry.title: SID configuration rules for SAP HANA DB and ABAP (Depends on same or different host)
entry.content:
Introduction

In SAP system landscapes, System ID (SID) plays a pivotal role in defining and managing individual system instances. The SID is a unique identifier assigned to each SAP system and is crucial for differentiating between various installations and instances. However, deciding on appropriate SID configurations can be challenging, especially when designing a complex landscape with multiple systems and hosts. The necessity of having distinct SIDs, whether on the same host or across different hosts, often sparks debate during SAP system planning.

 

Background

The challenge of SID configuration typically arises during the initial stages of SAP implementation. For instance, when deploying multiple SAP instances on a single host or across a cluster of servers, administrators often encounter issues with overlapping SIDs. These conflicts can cause disruptions in system communication, transport management, and monitoring. Furthermore, SAP notes such as SAP Note 2770989 and SAP Note 1953429 emphasize the importance of distinct SIDs, highlighting scenarios where improper SID planning leads to technical limitations and increased administrative overhead.

 

Discussion

Why SID Should Be Unique on the Same Host

On the same host, unique SIDs are mandatory to avoid conflicts between system directories, shared memory segments, and network ports. SAP systems rely on environment variables such as $SAPSYSTEMNAME and $SAPGLOBALHOST, which directly reference the SID. A duplicated SID on the same host could result in:

  • File System Overlap: SAP profiles and directories (e.g., /usr/sap/<SID>) would clash, leading to corruption or loss of critical files.
  • Port Conflicts: Services such as message servers and enqueue servers bind to specific ports tied to the SID. Duplicate SIDs could cause binding errors, preventing the system from starting.
  • Shared Memory Issues: Shared memory segments allocated for a specific SID cannot be reused, leading to unexpected system crashes.

 

Recommended Practice for Different Hosts

For different hosts, distinct SIDs are not strictly required but are highly recommended. This recommendation stems from general system administration principles and operational benefits:

  1. Simplified Monitoring: Unique SIDs make it easier to identify and monitor individual systems in centralized tools like SAP Solution Manager.
  2. Streamlined Transport Management: Transport directories and logs reference SIDs, reducing confusion during troubleshooting or audits.
  3. Disaster Recovery and Migration: Unique SIDs allow for easier replication and backup across systems without conflicting identifiers.

Example

Consider a scenario where both an SAP HANA database and an SAP NetWeaver AS ABAP system are deployed on the same server. If both systems were assigned the same SID, they would share critical directories (/usr/sap/<SID>), causing configuration overlaps. By assigning distinct SIDs (e.g., HDB for HANA and ERP for NetWeaver), administrators can segregate their environments effectively, as detailed in SAP Note 1953429.

 

Operational Benefits of Segregating SIDs

  1. Error Isolation: Troubleshooting system issues becomes more straightforward with clear identifiers for each environment.
  2. System Independence: Changes to one system (e.g., upgrades or patches) are isolated, minimizing risks to other environments.
  3. Audit and Compliance: Unique SIDs simplify compliance checks, as each system is distinctly identifiable in logs and reports.

 

Conclusion

Careful planning of SID assignments is a critical component of SAP landscape design. For systems hosted on the same server, unique SIDs are non-negotiable. For systems across different hosts, distinct SIDs, while not mandatory, greatly enhance manageability and operational efficiency. By adhering to SAP recommendations and guidelines, organizations can mitigate risks and ensure a robust, scalable SAP environment.

For additional guidance, refer to SAP Note 2770989 and SAP Note 1953429.

entry.title: Focused Build SP15: General Improvements in Generic Application
entry.content:
Introduction

With the release of the ST-OST Support Package 15 (SP15) for Focused Build in SAP Solution Manager, we have come up with some new and exciting features in the Generic application. The set of applications maintained under Generic Application consists of My Requirements, My Work Packages, My Work Items, My Defects, My Defect Corrections, My Change Documents and My Requests for Change.

Electronic Signature in Approval Tab for Generic Applications:

In Focused Build Applications, Electronic Signature can be activated in two different scenarios:

  1. For specific status changes
  2. Within the approval workflow

The Generic application already supports electronic signatures via status change, and this can be enabled through the below SPRO path:

SPRO ➡️ SAP Solution Manager ➡️ Focused Build ➡️ Change Control Management Extensions ➡️ Electronic Signature ➡️ Activate Signature for PPF Actions

With Support Package 15, we have expanded this functionality to include electronic signatures in approval steps. For Requirements and Request for Change documents, if the approval workflow is enabled, it is possible to activate electronic signatures at each step using the below SPRO path:

SPRO ➡️ SAP Solution Manager ➡️ Focused Build ➡️ Change Control Management Extensions ➡️ Electronic Signature ➡️ Activate Signature for Approval Assignment Block

Figure 1: Customizing to activate electronic signature in approval steps

DSIGNTYP* is the attribute name you can increment the number from 0 to 9, and for each number you may assign a transaction type. In this scenario, we have enabled this for Request for change and hence attribute name is DSIGNTYP1, and the value is the transaction type of Request for change i.e. S1CR.

When documents are in the “To be approved” status, approvers typically use the “Approve” or “Reject” buttons to quickly approve the document without navigating to the approval tab.

Figure 2:Quick access buttons – Approve and Reject when electronic signature is not active

 

However, with electronic signatures enabled, guidelines require that users see and acknowledge the specific approval steps they are signing. Therefore, the quick buttons on the footer will not be visible when electronic signatures are active. Instead, users will need to navigate to the Approval tab and edit the steps they intend to sign individually before saving the approval steps.

Figure 3: Save approval activity into the approval steps

After selecting the approval activity from the drop down and then clicking the save button, the electronic signature popup process is triggered, and only when the user signs the document, the status of the approval steps is updated and accordingly the status of the document, in our example it is the Requests for change.

Figure 4: Electronic signature popup

This enhancement ensures that the electronic signature process is reliable and compliant, providing clear visibility of the steps being approved.

Confirm current user as Message Processor for My Defects:

Previously, it was not possible for users to automatically have the current user as the Message Processor in My Defects application. This was only possible in the CRM Web UI client. With Support Pack 15, the users can confirm if they would like to be a Message Processor for the selected defect.

Figure 5: Add message processor dialog

After confirming, users would see partner details updated in Message Processor field of Business Partner section.

On click of “No” in the above popup, there is no change in Message Processor field.

Note: If customizing with parameter key IM_SUPP_TEAM_DISABLE in the view cluster AGS_WORK_CUSTOM is maintained as ‘X’, then there will be no dependency between the fields Message Processor and Support Team in Business Partner section. Hence, the Support Team field will still retain the previous value even though the current processor does not belong to it.

Otherwise, if IM_SUPP_TEAM_DISABLE does not contain any value, then there is a dependency between the field Message Processor and Support Team, and this would clear the value of Support Team if the current Message Processor entered does not belong to the same Support Team.

Navigating back from My Checklist Steps to Generic Application:

In the Checklists tab of Generic application, clicking on a checklist step navigates to the Details page of the My Checklists Step application.

Figure 6: Selecting a Checklist step
Figure 7: Navigation to Details page of My Checklist Steps after selecting a checklist step

However, clicking the back button navigated the users to the Details tab of the respective document. In alignment with the Fiori Design guidelines, this behavior is now corrected, and the users will be navigated to the Checklists tab in such a situation.

Figure 8: Back navigation from My Checklist Steps Details page

Linking a Defect to another Defect in the My Defects Application:

In the past, the Relations tab in My Defects application listed only the respective Defect Corrections.

With Support Pack 15, users are allowed to add dependent defects to a selected defect. A dependent defect is one that shares the same defect correction with another defect. This will help in reducing the creation of redundant defect corrections for different defects.

A dependent defect can be added by clicking the Add button in Relations tab.
Figure 9: Add button for adding a Dependent Defect

This will open a search dialog with where users can search for a dependent defect using multiple filter options.
Figure 10: Add dependent defect dialog with filter bar

The results are populated in the list where users can also navigate to the selected defect by clicking on the Defect ID. Users are allowed to add only one defect at a time. To add a selected defect, click on the Add Dependency button in the dialog footer.
Figure 11: Add dependency button to add a selected defect

Once the defect is added, it is populated in the list with a new relation type ‘is similar to’.
Figure 12: Selected defect gets added to the list of dependents

The current defect is also listed in the Relations tab of the added defect as a dependent defect.
Figure 13: The selected defect from previous step also shows the dependent defect

 

Usability improvements in the Generic Application:

With Support Pack 15, the Requested Release field is changed to a combo box that contains the Release Cycle description with a tooltip displaying the technical information about the Change Control Landscape, Release Type and Release Number. It is also enabled with case insensitive search functionality.

Figure 14: Requested release contains information about Change Control Landscape, Release Type and Release Number

Also, when adding dependency in Relations tab of My Work Packages in Generic application the Project field is changed to Combo box to have lesser clicks and respect Fiori UI5 usage guidelines.

Figure 15: Project filter is now a combo box

The above changes are also implemented in the Actual Release and Project fields in the Mass Change Operations and Requirements Management application respectively.

Please feel free to leave feedback in the comment section or submit any questions you may have on this page. You are also encouraged to follow SAP Solution Manager and Focused Build for SAP Solution Manager to stay updated with the new features and improvements.

Important Links and Resources

  • Usage Rights
  • Learn how SAP Activate and Focused Build with SAP Solution Manager is working together
  • ALM Community page

entry.title: SAP Asset Performance Management in Maritime Industry
entry.content:
As a Marine Engineer with years of experience working on ships, I've seen firsthand the challenges of maintaining complex machinery in harsh maritime environments. One solution that stands out for its potential to revolutionize ship maintenance is SAP Asset Performance Management (APM). By leveraging SAP APM, we can significantly enhance the efficiency of our machines and reduce maintenance costs.

A ship presents an excellent use case for SAP APM due to its repetitive operations and the concept of sister ships. Typically, sister ships have identical machinery make and model, allowing for the creation of a standardized template for onboard machinery. This template can be directly applied to sister ships and, with minor adjustments, across the entire fleet. Such a template enhances efficiency and significantly reduces the time required to implement SAP APM and S4 Enterprise Asset Management (EAM).

The foundational structure can be established in S4, with initial strategies derived from OEM instruction manuals or the Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) of the owners or management company, based on their extensive experience with the machinery. This approach also facilitates learning from issues encountered on one ship, thereby preventing similar problems on other ships.

Modern ships are outfitted with numerous sensors and data points, and regular logbook entries can be directly input into S4. This combination of sensor data and manual entries allows for the easy maintenance of time series data, which can be synced with the Internet of Things (IoT) to generate trends and predict failures or anomalous behavior. Connectivity at sea is no longer an issue, thanks to VSAT and Starlink, which provide global internet coverage, even in open seas and international waters.

SAP APM is a leading cloud-based SaaS product designed to monitor the overall performance of machinery. It falls under the IAM/EAM umbrella and is applicable across any industry where machinery maintenance is essential. SAP APM seamlessly integrates with S4 EAM (both cloud and on-premise) for planning and scheduling, and with mobility solutions like SAP Service and Asset Management (SSAM) for execution and feedback.

SAP APM is a unique solution which brings together multiple important functions required for an effective and mature maintenance management. It can be broadly divided into two functions-

  1. Asset Strategy
  2. Asset Health

These components of SAP APM can further be divided into different applications and processes as mentioned below-

1. Asset Strategy:

    1. R&C- Risk and Criticality
    2. SAC- Strategy Assessment for Class
    3. RCM- Reliability Centered Maintenance
    4. FMEA- Failure Mode and Effect Analysis
    5. Recommendation

2. Asset Health:

    1. Inspections
    2. Indicator Monitoring
    3. Rules and Alerts
    4. Failure Curve Analytics
    5. IoT

Asset strategies can easily be defined and reused across the ships in the fleet as the operating conditions and parameters will be very similar in similar types of ships. The comparisons can then be made across ships to finetune the strategy by learning from each other’s data and observations.

Reliability engineers can optimize and align maintenance strategies by carefully evaluating operating conditions and machinery types based on their criticality. They can recommend proactive, reactive, and improvement tasks for equipment with similar characteristics. These recommendations can then be converted into task lists and maintenance plans, generating maintenance orders to execute the tasks. Continuous feedback and monitoring help adjust these recommendations, potentially saving millions of dollars by avoiding unnecessary maintenance costs in spare parts, maintenance crew, and downtime required for routine maintenance tasks.

Asset health can be monitored by evaluating indicator values from S4 measurement documents or sensors. These readings can be synced with IoT, allowing time series data to be analyzed for machine performance and failure prediction. This proactive approach helps prevent machinery breakdowns and plant shutdowns, potentially saving millions of dollars in maintenance costs, unplanned material, and service procurement. 

SAP APM offers several benefits for the maritime industry:

  • Risk & Criticality Assessment: This feature helps identify critical equipment, allowing for decisions on whether to implement a rigorous maintenance schedule or run the machinery to failure.
  • Trend Analysis: The vast amount of data generated from multiple ships can enhance machine learning models, leading to more accurate failure predictions.
  • Asset Health Monitoring: By establishing rules to track parameters such as vibration, temperature, and pressure, alerts can be generated when these parameters exceed defined limits.
  • Strategy Assessment: Developing strategies for equipment classes can significantly reduce maintenance costs by avoiding unnecessary repetitive maintenance.
  • Failure Curve Analysis: This tool predicts the remaining useful life of equipment, enabling informed decisions on when to perform maintenance.

 

Stay tuned! In my upcoming blogs, I'll delve into specific use cases for SAP APM.

I’ll update this blog with links to the future posts.

 

Useful links:

IAM- Intelligent Asset Management: This is a suite of cloud and on-premise solutions such as SAP APM and S4 EAM focused on the maintenance of machinery in simple terms.

SAP Asset Performance Management (APM): This is a cloud product from SAP to help reliability engineers.

S4 EAM- Enterprise Asset Management: This is SAP’s solution for maintenance initiation, planning, scheduling, execution and closure integrated with materials management and finance.

IoT- Internet of Things: IoT is a network which collects data from physical devices as a time series for analysis and tracking of trends to predict behavior.

 

FAQs:

1. Is SAP APM a cloud or on-premise solution?

  • SAP APM is a cloud product which regularly receives new updates and features. It is true cloud product which is continuously evolving and improving based on customer feedback and SAP’s innovation.

2. Can SAP APM be implemented as a stand-alone system?

  • It cannot be implemented as a stand-alone system. It needs a backend S$ EAM system to replicate and sync the master data.

3. Can SAP APM be integrated with S4 EAM’s cloud version or on-premise version?

  • It can be integrated with both cloud and on-premise versions with certain pre-requisites.

4. Can SAP APM be integrated with SAP ECC?

  • There are certain minimum requirements with which SAP APM can be integrated with SAP ERP as well. SAP Mobile Add-On is a prerequisite for the integration of SAP Asset Performance Management with the SAP ERP system. The minimum requirement for using the latest SAP Asset Performance Management integration features are as follows:
    • Release Version: SAP ERP: Mobile Add-On for ERP 6.3 SP11
    • Minimum Requirement: SAP ERP: ECC 6 EHP7 SP14

You can find additional details on this link- https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_APM/d70aaf7b5a2543fea6b7ffd952500e78/45db992d0cf544dd97d11616753e30a9.html

entry.title: My Inbox: Integration of SAPUI5 Fiori Applications, embed into MyInbox
entry.content:
Purpose:

A customer request came in the last few days. The request was to show another Fiori application in a message coming to MyInbox from an existing workflow. As a result of the research I have done in this context for about 5 days. In general, I could not find a practical and easy application from SAP's blogs, including SAP's snotes. There were many solutions from writing a new MyInbox extension to writing a new Fiori application. However, what I wanted was a very simple request. I have an existing working MyInbox, a workflow and a ready Fiori application. I had to adapt them to work with each other.

If we schematize it to understand better:

Our inbox looks like this:

What is requested is to display the Fiori application in MyInbox Content:

 

ASSUMPTIONS and REQUIRED:

1. An existing working MyInbox.

If not, you can check the following link:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-fiori-for-sap-s-4hana-fiori-my-inbox-part-1-activation/ba-p/13326175

2. Check My Inbox version:

Minimum required version of My Inbox:1.23.43,1.27.33, 1.31.33,100.35.25,100.39.11.

(This embedding type is not supported with My Inbox version 2.5.x.)

Once you enter My Inbox, you can check it from the menu on the top right.

3. There must be a working workflow and it must be able to send the message to Myinbox without any problems.

4. There must be a working Fiori Application that we want to show.

 

Solution:

I assume you have a myinbox that looks like this.



1) First, open the Fiori Launchpad Designer with the /UI2/FLPD_CUST tcode and check if your MyInbox application has "allItems=true" in its Parameters.

2) Then we add a new Action to the Fiori application that we want to show in the Fiori Launchpad Designer. An existing working action can be copied and modified. Or you can also customize the working action.

openMode is added as a parameter, Obligaratorisch is selected and embedIntoDetailsNestedRouter is given as the value.

3) Then we go to the Backend and go to the SWFVISU tcode. We add our task with the task no and select the "Intent Base Navigation" option. Then we give the values ​​of our Fiori Application as parameters to its content. 

 

And:

 

References:

https://me.sap.com/notes/2305401/E
https://me.sap.com/notes/0003347852
https://me.sap.com/notes/3436302

entry.title: SAP S4 HANA PUBLIC CLOUD - WHAT IS IT ABOUT?
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Explained in Detail

SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud is a robust, cloud-based enterprise resource planning (ERP) solution designed to enhance and streamline business processes across various industries. 

I am currently working as a SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud Architect in the Public Sector industry and responsible for the Architecture, Design, and implementation for a large Public sector organization and can see tremendous advantages in why the Public sector would benefit from the SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud edition. In my current project, we are implementing SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud 2408 utilizing the Central Business Configuration (CBC) tool to activate the specified industry relevant business processes.  Some of the Core Features offered by SAP S/4 HANA Public cloud solution that we are seeing in the project are as follows:

1. It comes with a Pre-Configured System: SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud has pre-defined business processes that we can activate and implement. This allows organizations to adapt to the standard processes easily and without requiring any enhancements. With SAP's Central Business in place to support this, organizations can have their business processes configured from one single tool in the cloud. Central Business Configuration for their implementation. As Cloud is becoming much advanced with the use of AI & ML capabilities, public sector organizations such as UK Councils are seeing the benefits in the product and have started their journey by implementation of the SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud solution to transform their existing landscape. 

2. Endless Scalability and Flexibility to activate new processes : Organizations can now activate business processes or use new add on features much easily than before. The new solution provides in depth insights related to how the process works with the help of Signavio and allows businesses to be in control of their system like never before. 

3.  Innovation : SAP keeps pushing regular updates and also keep advancing the functionalities that it offers with the latest use of AI & ML. Fiori apps are like never before, as it integrates with AI capabilities providing better view of the reporting and also enabling business users to easily learn how AI is redefining the business processes. 

4. Faster Integration :  SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud integrates much faster and effectively with other systems whether it be an existing SAP system or any other ERP or Non ERP system. SAP provides built in and readily available API's that helps organizations transition their data exchange smoothly. 

5. Impact on Environment and how AI is helping achieve Sustainability : SAP's S/4 HANA Public cloud offeres various new AI enabled features that now allows organizations to keep track of their carbon emissions and how their business processes are affecting the environment in todays fasted paced world.  With new reporting capabilities such as SAP Analytics Cloud, business can meet their sustainability goals much effectively and helps them to make better decisions in the longer run. 

As the product is getting matured further with AI and ML capabilities, it is fair to say that SAP's S/4 HANA Public Cloud has enormous benefits to offer to the Public Sector. 

I will continue to share insights from my existing project and how we can leverage the key benefits from SAP's latest offerings and AI enabled capabilities.

entry.title: Monitoring Integration Issues with the New “Issues Detected” Event in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
SAP's cloud transformation strategy involves breaking down monolithic applications into interconnected yet independent cloud services. It is crucial to ensure that customers don't lose end-to-end transparency or visibility during this integration process. To achieve this, customers need the flexibility to configure events for integration issues based on specific statuses, enabling quick resolution of any business impacts.

The introduction of the new "Issues detected" event in SAP Cloud ALM's Integration & Exception Monitoring app gives customers greater flexibility in configuring such events. This new event is available for all monitored services and systems.

In this blog, we'll use monitoring of  SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration) using SAP Cloud ALM as an example. As part of monitoring, we have enabled the monitoring to collect all “SAP Integration Suite Messages” which could be in various status groups like, Error, Warning, Cancelled etc.

Issues Detected Metric:

By default, the metric “Issues detected” is defined to raise events for messages in “Error” or “Warning” status group.

The following can be configured by the users: 

Metric Parameter:

  • Frequency of event calculation: The event calculation is a recurring process that happens every 5 minutes. Currently, this interval is not configurable.
  • Messages older than n minutes: When an event calculation process starts, we consider all those messages which has remained in the specific status group from more than ‘n’ minutes.
  • Number of Events per calculation: This parameter allows you to set the maximum number of events that can be generated in one calculation, with a default value of 50. If the number of unique events during the current calculation is less than 50, individual events are raised. If the number exceeds 50, a summary event will be raised.

Filter:

In this section, customers can set specific filters for event configuration. By default, for the  "Issues detected" metric, the "Status Group" includes "Error" and " Warning". Thus, if customers opt for the default values, this event will be triggered when there are messages with "Error" or "Warning" status group. Customers have the flexibility to modify the filter by adding or removing more statuses or any other filter parameters.

Grouping:

This section can be used to decide how the events shall be grouped before it is raised. The customer can choose grouping based on multiple attributes.

Event Actions:

This section can be used to configure the actions to be initiated when an event is raised for this event configuration. By default, creation of alerts in turned on.

Sample Scenario:

Customer Need

Suppose the customer wishes to set up an event-raising configuration under the following conditions:

  • All messages with a status of FAILED, ESCALATED, or PROCESSING.
  • Messages that remain in the specified status for more than 15 minutes.
  • An event should be generated for every individual message that meets these conditions.
  • No more than 10 events should be generated during each calculation period (5 minutes).
  • Email notifications should be sent to specific users.

Customer Configuration

To fulfil the above requirement, the customer can set up the metric "Issues Detected" with the following configuration:

  • In the Filter section:
    • Remove the prefilled values for the status group and add the required values for status: FAILED, ESCALATED, PROCESSING.
    • Note: "Status" refers to the status of messages in the local cloud service. The status group maps these statuses to a specific predefined status group in SAP Cloud ALM. For example, the FAILED status in Cloud Integration is mapped to the Status Group "Error" in SAP Cloud ALM.
  • In the Parameter section:
    •  Set "Messages older than n minutes" to 15 minutes.
    •  Set "Number of events per calculation" to 10.
  • In the Grouping section:
    • Configure the parameters to include "Artifact Name", "Status", "Status Text", and "Message ID".
  • In the Event Action section:
    • Enable the email notification option and add the recipient emails.

How the configuration unfolds

Imagine a scenario where we have 5 messages with FAILED status, as shown below.

The following process occurs:

  1. According to the configuration, the application will search for all messages that have been in the FAILED status for more than 15 minutes. There are 5 such messages.
  2. The application groups these messages as per the configuration.  Since the grouping includes the Message ID as a parameter, and each message has a unique Message ID, we will end up with 5 distinct events.
  3. Given that the number of events (5) is less than the "number of events per calculation" (10), 5 events will be triggered.
  4. The user will receive 5 emails, one for each message ID.

For more information on how to configure these settings, refer to Setting Up Events in SAP Help Portal

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM Roadshow LAC 2025
entry.content:
Join Us for the SAP Cloud ALM Roadshow LAC 2025 in Mexico!

 

After successful roadshows in São Paulo (Brazil) on November 11, 2024, and Buenos Aires (Argentina) on November 19, 2024, we’re excited to bring the SAP Cloud ALM Roadshow LAC to Monterrey (Mexico) on January 29, 2025! This event will be a fantastic opportunity to learn about the latest in SAP Cloud Application Lifecycle Management (Cloud ALM) and discover the tools and strategies that can help you streamline your projects.

Whether you’ve already attended one of our previous roadshows or are joining us for the first time, this event is packed with valuable insights, hands-on workshops, and networking opportunities that you won’t want to miss!

What is SAP Cloud ALM?

SAP Cloud Application Lifecycle Management (Cloud ALM) provides a comprehensive suite of cloud-based tools to support the entire lifecycle of your applications — from development to deployment, monitoring, and continuous optimization. With SAP Cloud ALM, organizations can improve efficiency, reduce risks, and ensure successful project execution, all while fostering collaboration across teams.

Why Attend the SAP Cloud ALM Roadshow LAC?

  1. Exclusive Insights into SAP Cloud ALM Tools: Gain in-depth knowledge of the latest SAP Cloud ALM innovations and strategies to improve your application lifecycle.
  2. Region-Specific Solutions: Learn how SAP Cloud ALM addresses the unique challenges businesses face in Latin America building on the success of our Brazil and Argentina roadshows.
  3. Networking Opportunities: Connect with SAP experts, peers, and industry leaders in the region. Share experiences, ask questions, and build relationships with other professionals.

Key Topics We Will Cover:

At this year’s SAP Cloud ALM Roadshow LAC, we’ll be covering a wide range of crucial topics that will help you optimize your SAP environment:

  • SAP Cloud ALM for RISE with SAP and Clean Core Support Strategies: Understand how SAP Cloud ALM integrates with RISE with SAP and supports Clean Core strategies, enabling smoother migrations and system optimizations.
  • Implementing Intelligent Enterprise with SAP Cloud ALM: Learn how SAP Cloud ALM can be leveraged to implement an Intelligent Enterprise, allowing businesses to align their SAP systems with future-proof digital transformation strategies.
  • Automating Testing with Tricentis: Discover how Tricentis' advanced testing automation can accelerate your SAP project cycles, reduce risks, and enhance quality assurance across the board.
  • Optimizing Operations Combining AI and SAP Cloud ALM: Explore how AI-powered solutions within SAP Cloud ALM can enhance your operational efficiency, reduce downtime, and improve decision-making.
  • Leveraging SAP Focused Run for Advanced Monitoring Capabilities: Gain insights into using SAP Focused Run to enable advanced monitoring, real-time analytics, and proactive issue resolution across your SAP landscape.
  • Transitioning from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM: Get a step-by-step guide on how to successfully transition from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM, including key benefits and best practices to ensure a seamless migration.

Event Details:

Date: January 29, 2025

Location: Ricardo Margain 335, Torre 1 - Piso 16;
Col. Valle del Campestre, San Pedro Garza García,
N.L., México - CP 66265

Time: Half-day event, including networking and consultation opportunities

Register Now!

Seats are limited, and you won’t want to miss out on this valuable learning experience. Register now to secure your spot at the SAP Cloud ALM Roadshow LAC 2025 in Mexico!

https://events.sap.com/sap-alm-roadshow-na/en/home

We look forward to welcoming you on January 29, 2025, and sharing how SAP Cloud ALM can transform your business.

entry.title: From History to Innovation: Analyzing Custom SAP System Evolution - A Primer
entry.content:
A Glimpse into the History of Custom SAP Systems

Consider the transport history of a small to medium-sized enterprise (SME). This record captures essential moments in the system's life - when transports occurred, who approved them, and which objects were moved into production. Such insights provide a clear picture of how the system has developed over time.

Example of ERP system workbench transport history (SME customer)Example of ERP system customizing transport history (same SME customer)

The volume of transported objects varies with company size. Larger enterprises often show a higher density of daily transports, reflecting more extensive system usage:

Another customer example of an SAP ECC workbench transport history. The blue hatched area represents the density of the daily transports as additional information 

It becomes more interesting when we take the transport history of a software package (DEVLASS) as an example for this customer and plot the individual developers and their activities:

An example softwarepackage (DEVLASS) workbench transport history of this customer

Each horizontal line represents the activity of a user within the software package (DEVLASS). The names of the users are grayed out for privacy reasons)

When looking at the activities based on the workbench transport history and considering the time-limited activities of users along with their simultaneous or staggered actions, we can discuss the development history and the developers involved. This makes for a good conversation starter with the relevant development manager, system administrator, or subject matter expert: "Which developer is the most experienced?", "Was knowledge able to be passed on between developers?", "Can I identify development initiatives?", "These two developers are our best procurement process experts!"

The way this information is presented is well-suited for storytelling.

 

Storytelling and Reconstructed History

Storytelling transforms complex technical processes into relatable narratives. Instead of drowning stakeholders in dry details, the narrative approach fosters collaboration between IT teams and business stakeholders. When technical information is presented as a story, IT professionals can communicate their ideas more effectively, leading to richer discussions about system features and enhancements.

However, storytelling comes with its challenges. One risk is exaggeration or misrepresentation; presenting hypothetical scenarios as facts can create misunderstandings and unrealistic expectations. To maintain trust, it’s crucial that stories are grounded in real data.

 

Understanding the Evolution of Custom SAP Systems for Better Transformation

Every custom SAP system tells a story shaped by organizational decisions throughout its development. This narrative encompasses not just technical specifications but also the experiences of those involved. Analyzing this evolution helps organizations make informed decisions about their software strategies.

Having spent years in Custom Development and Innovative Business Solutions (IBSO), I’ve seen firsthand how important it is to examine software evolution. The method of Software Evolution Analysis arose from the need for quick communication with clients about custom systems. This analysis captures the essence of a system's history, showing how past choices influence current functions and future developments.

In future blog posts, I will go deeper into this topic, sharing examples and insights that demonstrate how storytelling can clarify the complexities of custom SAP systems. 

 

References

  1. Change and Transport System (CTS): https://support.sap.com/en/tools/software-logistics-tools/change-and-transport-system.html 
  2. Lehman, M. M., & Belady, L. (1974). "Program Evolution: Processes of Software Change."  (remark: The article by Lehman was published in 1974, just two years after the founding of SAP in 1972 by former IBM employees.); Lehman, M. M. (1980). "Programs, Life Cycles, and the Laws of Software Evolution." In Proceedings of the IEEE.
  3. Create Meaning by Storytelling: Fisher, Walter R. (1985). The Narrative Paradigm: An Elaboration. Communication Monographs, 51(4), 347-67. https://doi.org/10.1080/03637758509376117; Rooney, T., Lawlor, K., & Rohan, E. (2016). Telling Tales: Storytelling as a Methodological Approach in Research. The Electronic Journal of Business Research Methods, 14(2), 147-156. https://academic-publishing.org/index.php/ejbrm/article/download/1349/1312/1345 ; Woods, S., et al. (2021). Storytelling as a Research Tool Used to Explore Insights and as an Intervention. PMC. https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC8592844/

entry.title: GTT (global temporary tables) in ABAP-development
entry.content:
What is GTT?

In databases, Temporary table - is an object on database level, where data can be stored after select-operation; however data are stored during session only and not transferred from database to application. It gives option to apply SQL-operators to "temporary data" for calculations and do not fullfill extra-roundtrip between database and application servers. In terms of ABAP-development we could see GTT as alternative to "temporary internal tables" and UNION-operators.

GTT is a transparent table of special type in ABAP, which can store data during DB LUW and the data could be read only during the current session. At the beginning and at the end of session the table should be cleared implicitly (via DELETE FROM operator). The main purposes of GTT is to divide some complex selection on the several steps. GTT incurs lower administration costs than regular transparent tables and provide higher performance. We can say that GTT is like internal table but on the database side. Let us see demo-sample.

Demo sample for GTT (global temporary table)

GTT is really useful when we need to read data from different database-sources which has different purposes, but can be transformed to the same structure. Assume, we need to read list of materials (MATNR), which was specified in orders on some DATE1 and in invoices in some invoice date DATE1. 

For the sake of demonstration I will create 4 custom-table (they would be like VBAK, VBAP, VBRK, VBRP, but far shortly).

This is huge simplified structure of VB*-sales tab, but for demo sample it is suitable. The main idea is that data are in different tabs. Then I will fill data with help of utility from here (github link). And now let's specify how we can solve the task above:

1. We can read data from every table (or join) and unite records on the application server side into one result table (it can be called classic way, so I will not pay much attention to this approach here).

2. We can use UNION from two JOINs and receive all data. Potential implementation could be found here. 

3. We can use Global Temporary Table (GTT). We select from ORDER-tables and insert into GTT on database side without roundtrip to application server; then we select from INVOICE-tables and insert into GTT and the final step we read from GTT the final result. Implementation could be found here.

How to create GTT in SAP Net Weaver / DDIC

Let's see step by step how to create GTT for the demo.

The GTT created via tcode SE11 as transparent table, but we put mark that it is temporary table. We should specify L as delivered class.

 Then we specify fields as usual

Then we are go by menu: Extras -> Change/Display Table Category

And put Table Category as Global Temporary table

Technical settings are not maintained for GTT (data class, size category etc.). Then we should activate GTT in data dictionary and it is done 🙂 . Limitations for GTT are described in abap-help.

Using GTT and reading data with GTT

The full implementation of the approach is here.

GTT allows us divide the data-selections on step-by-step approach. So we could split it into two abap-methods: _fill_gtt_from_order and _fill_gtt_from_invoice. So, it increase readability; also we could pay attention to the system variable sy-dbcnt and we can know how many records on each step was inserted into GTT. I want to highlight that we are not selecting into GTT, but we are inserting into GTT (we are doing INSERT INTO ZTC8A016_MAT_TMP).

  METHOD _fill_gtt_from_order.
    INSERT ztc8a016_mat_tmp FROM
     (
     SELECT DISTINCT
      'O' AS src_type,
       ordi~matnr AS matnr
        FROM ztc8a016_ordi AS ordi
        JOIN ztc8a016_ordh AS ordh ON ordi~vbeln EQ ordh~vbeln
      WHERE ordh~order_date EQ @MV_trg_date
     ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD _fill_gtt_from_invoice.
    INSERT ztc8a016_mat_tmp FROM
     (
        SELECT DISTINCT
          'I' AS src_type,
          invi~matnr AS matnr
        FROM ztc8a016_invi AS invi
        JOIN ztc8a016_invh AS invh ON invi~inv_num EQ invh~inv_num
      WHERE invh~invoice_date EQ @MV_trg_date
     ).
  ENDMETHOD.

In case we need to add additional source (it can be: deliveries, purchasing docs etc), then we DO NOT NEED to change the current SELECT-operators, we will add additional abap-methods. Therefore, it is more readable and changeable approach.

After all insertions into GTT, we can read data from it. And it is important that we could apply almost any SQL-operators to it, including aggregated functions, JOINs, WHERE/HAVING etc. However after using GTT, we should keep it clear; there should not be any commit (including implicit).

    _fill_gtt_from_order( ).

    _fill_gtt_from_invoice( ).

    """"""""" read
    " we can use almost any SQL-operators
    SELECT DISTINCT matnr
        FROM ztc8a016_mat_tmp
      ORDER BY matnr DESCENDING
      INTO TABLE @ET_matnr_list
      UP TO 3 ROWS.

    IF mv_mode EQ '3'.
      " if GTT is not empty - we will get RunTime Error (aka red Dump)
      " implicit commit via message
      MESSAGE i000(cl) WITH 'Runtime Error goes here'.
    ENDIF.

    """"""""" delete - MUST EXIST
    DELETE FROM ztc8a016_mat_tmp.

    MESSAGE i000(cl) WITH 'GTT is empty'.

Full example is here for demo. ABAP-help for GTT is here.

Conclusion

Advantages of GTT:

1) we could avoid extra roundtrips with large amounts of data between application server and database server

2) we could divide select-logic into abap-methods and it does improve readability

3) because of low administrative costs the table has improved performance than temporary table.

entry.title: Sustainability and Ethical Sourcing in Procurement
entry.content:
Imagine a future where every product we use helps make the world a better place. This vision is becoming real as more companies adopt sustainable and ethical procurement practices. By making smart choices about what they buy, companies can have a big impact on the environment and society.

Why is Sustainability in Procurement Important?

Sustainability in procurement means making smart choices that help the Earth. This includes thinking about how much energy a product uses, if it can be recycled, and its overall impact on the environment. Sustainable procurement not only protects the environment but also improves a company's reputation, efficiency, and innovation. Companies that focus on sustainability often save money, attract top talent, and build stronger relationships with stakeholders.

The Importance of Environmental Impact

Choosing eco-friendly products and materials is crucial. For example, a furniture company aims to use only recycled and renewable materials by 2030, which will greatly reduce its environmental footprint. Sustainable procurement helps companies follow environmental laws and avoid legal problems, as seen with the European Union's guidelines for green purchasing.

Cost Savings and Consumer Behavior

Sustainable products can lead to significant cost savings. Energy-efficient products lower energy bills, and reducing waste cuts disposal costs. Additionally, more consumers are choosing environmentally friendly products. A survey showed that 73% of consumers are willing to change their habits to reduce their environmental impact. Companies that respond to these preferences can gain a competitive advantage and build brand loyalty.

Ethical Sourcing and Social Responsibility

Ethical sourcing ensures that products are made under fair conditions, including fair pay and safe working environments. This practice supports good labor practices and helps communities. For instance, an outdoor clothing company has been a leader in ethical sourcing, ensuring their supply chain follows the highest labor standards.

Implementing Sustainable and Ethical Procurement

To implement sustainable and ethical procurement, companies should set clear sustainability goals, create policies, engage stakeholders, evaluate environmental impact, and conduct regular audits. Training procurement teams and working with suppliers are also important steps. Technological innovations like AI and IoT can further enhance transparency and traceability in supply chains.

Connecting to SAP Ariba and SAP Procurement Solutions

SAP provides integrated solutions that help manage sustainability and ethical sourcing throughout procurement processes. SAP Ariba, for instance, enables businesses to connect with a global network of suppliers who follow sustainable and ethical standards. It helps assess suppliers and ensures they meet a company’s sustainability criteria. Additionally, SAP Ariba's advanced analytics and reporting tools allow organizations to monitor and improve their procurement practices continuously.

The SAP Business Network connects buyers and suppliers on a global scale, promoting transparency and collaboration. It supports sustainable and ethical procurement by providing a platform where companies can share best practices, innovation, and compliance information. This network enhances supply chain resilience and operational efficiency, contributing to the overall sustainability goals of the organization.

Future Trends in Sustainable Procurement

Looking ahead, several trends will shape the future of sustainable procurement. These include stricter environmental regulations, changes in consumer expectations, a focus on the circular economy, and advancements in technology. Companies that embrace these trends will thrive in today's market.

Conclusion

Sustainability and ethical sourcing are essential for responsible and forward-thinking organizations. By adopting these practices, companies can help create a sustainable and fair world while enjoying significant business benefits. As more people care about the environment and social issues, companies that embrace sustainability will thrive.

Best regards

Lars

entry.title: Best Practices for SAP BTP ABAP Environment
entry.content:
Best Practices: SAP BTP ABAP Environment

Introduction: 

This blog post summarizes the best practices for getting started with the SAP BTP ABAP environment (aka Steampunk). Further articles and blog posts are linked in the appropriate places so that more information is available there if required.  

The SAP BTP ABAP environment was developed to create cloud-enabled business applications, services and extensions. It offers the ABAP cloud development model that uses SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Fiori and a cloud-optimized ABAP language with standard APIs. Developers can use a toolset for tight integration with Git-enabled lifecycle management. 

The use of SAP BTP ABAP environment offers 3 key benefits:

  • It enables a side-by-side extension option for the custom extension of SAP S/4HANA
  • The cloud transformation can be accelerated by using reusable ABAP assets to create innovative solutions for the cloud
  • Keep the core clean, the on-premise custom code is kept on another decoupled platform, where you can profit from independent and regular release cycles

The following graphic shows the four different areas of use of the SAP BTP ABAP environment. Individual adaptations for each interest group are possible for each part so that the best possible benefit is achieved. 

SAP BTP ABAP Environment Positioning

Development

You can simply connect the ABAP Cloud system to Eclipse so that you can use the ABAP Development Tools (ADT) provided there to develop the ABAP Cloud apps. Build your own OData services using the ABAP RESTful application programming model optimized for SAP HANA Cloud. You can also analyze the ABAP code with advanced custom code migration tools and evaluate the effects of conversion to SAP S/4HANA, migration to SAP BTP or during the clean core journey. 

Administration and Integration

Administrative tasks such as user management and security setup can be performed via the role-based SAP Fiori Launchpad. Create extension projects between ABAP Cloud and other SAP Build tools, and easily integrate with existing on-premises systems and any SAP S/4HANA systems. You can also use third-party services that are based on HTTP. 

The central aspect of the SAP BTP ABAP environment is the broad range of data centers globally provided. You can choose from a total number of more than 20 data centers, a list of all of them can be found in the SAP Discovery Center. The number and therefore the variety will continue to increase, and further locations are planned and will soon be available. All other upcoming features can be found in the roadmap and the what's new page for the SAP BTP ABAP environment in the SAP Help Portal. In addition, there is already a blog post in which the upgrade window for next year's releases is listed and you can find information regarding the Cloud System Notification Subscriptions (CSNS).

The Landscape Portal is part of the SAP BTP ABAP environment offering and acts as a central tool to perform system administration (for example system hibernation), manage lifecycle operations, and facilitate the configuration and deployment of multitenant SaaS solutions.  

Choosing Your Starting Point: The Free Tier Option 

It is important to note at the initial stage that it is not necessary to create all the systems at once, but only those that are needed at the time. On the one hand, this ensures clarity and, on the other, saves unnecessary costs.  

We recommend using the Free Tier option for your first contact with this system. This allows you to try out everything free of charge and then decide whether you want to continue using it. This offer can be used for a maximum of 90 days. 

Setting Up Your System: Step-by-Step Guide 

The next step is the creation of a software component as a delivery unit for your project, this is possible via the SAP Fiori Launchpad. The free tier system can be converted into a standard plan development system, so that all projects and created files can be transferred. It is of course also possible to create a development system directly. The SAP BTP Cockpit is generally used for the system creation. 

With the ABAP Development Tools (ADT) for Eclipse, all items can be developed as usual and released using the transport requests. 

Once development is complete, a test system can be created via the SAP BTP Cockpit. The software components can then be pulled into the test system in the SAP Fiori Launchpad. 

Best Practices for System Management  

A subaccount to create a system is required. We recommend creating a separate subaccount for each system. This ensures maximum flexibility, for example with the trust settings. All information about Cloud Foundry and the required accounts and spaces can be found in the SAP Help Portal.

The next step is to define a region in which the system is located. There are three different hyperscalers to choose from, which are represented in more than 20 data centers worldwide. 

And each system must be assigned to a Cloud Foundry Space. Here too, it is recommended to use a Cloud Foundry Space for only one system to create a better overview. 

Optimizing Costs: Sizing and Hibernation Tips  

All systems should initially be created with the minimum size, one ACU (ABAP Compute Unit) and 2 HCUs (HANA Compute Unit). This usually provides sufficient coverage, as one ACU can serve around 1,000 active business users per day. These are the only two metrics that are relevant for this. The size can of course be easily changed and adapted at a later stage vie the SAP BTP Cockpit. An overview of the prices can be found in the SAP Discovery Center, where the ACUs and HCUs are also explained in more detail. 

If the system has more than just the minimum size available, it is possible to use the elastic scaling. The resources are adjusted and automatically scaled based on the required workload. To be able to use this functionality, the BTPEA Commercial Model is required. 

The default setting when creating a system is a development system, but if you want to create a test or productive system, you must uncheck this box. To distinguish between the different systems, you can assign them a descriptive ID in the “ABAP System ID” field. 

The system hibernation function can be used to save further costs. This function is suitable for stopping development systems outside working hours, for example at weekends, starting test systems only when they are needed or starting the productive system only when it goes live. It can simply be started up via the Landscape Portal. 

A detailed cost analysis for customers can be found in this blog post. 

Recommended System Landscapes: Finding the Right Fit 

The various landscapes are only briefly explained here, detailed descriptions can be found in the documentation. When setting up the systems, we recommend that you only start with the system that is currently required. It is not necessary to set up the entire system landscape directly. It is sufficient if only the development system is set up first and then the test or production system as soon as the need is met. On the one hand, this provides a better overview at the beginning and on the other hand, costs can be reduced. 

3-System Landscape 

This landscape consists of a development system, a quality assurance system and the production system. This allows the tests of new developments to be tested directly in parallel and ensures that the application is also executable in non-development systems. 

5 System Landscape 

In this scenario, the code lines for development and corrections can be handled separately. The correction system and the test system are on the same level as the production system. This means that the respective corrections can be tested before they are imported into the production system. To develop larger releases and code parts, there is an actual development system. There is also the corresponding test system. Urgent corrections can be implemented directly and have no influence on the normal development cycle. 

Note: At this point you can use the system hibernation function, because if it is not necessary to enter corrections frequently, the correction system and the associated test system can be shut down and thus also save costs. If there is a need for these systems, they can simply be started up again. A detailed explanation of the system hibernation can be found in this blog post. 

This learning journey  can give you an overview of how to create an ABAP environment on the SAP BTP or you can attend our monthly Customer and Partner Roundtable. Among other topics, new product features are presented in this meeting, live demos are shown by experts or you have the opportunity to ask questions live. To register for the roundtable, please follow the SAP Community for SAP BTP ABAP Environment, where the next date will be announced in a blog post and registration details will be provided. If you have missed a roundtable or would like to look at past ones again, you can find them on GitHub, where the respective slides are also available. 

Hopefully this blog post has given you a clear introduction to the SAP BTP ABAP environment.

entry.title: Santa's Early Gift for SAP BTP
entry.content:
At this time of year, as many of us are looking forward to some fun over the festive period, I like many people am busy wrapping presents for friends and family. But in the fine spirit of giving, the rather nice team at G2, playing the role of Santa in their smart red and white livery, have given the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) team an early Christmas gift, in the form of Winter 2025 badges, celebrating SAP BTP’s recognition as a Leader across a number of different categories in the G2 Winter 2025 reports. This includes a Momentum badge this time around too, indicating that our customers place us in the top 25% of leaders in certain product categories. What a nice surprise for the team, and a great way to round out 2024!

The recognition in new G2 reports builds on that received just a few short months ago in their Fall 2024 reports, and shows a continued appreciation from SAP’s customers for the value they derive from using SAP BTP, as badges are offered only following a robust scoring methodology which ranks vendor solutions based on real, verified, and trusted software buyers’ reviews. This of course gives the SAP BTP team a great feeling of pride, and gratitude too, knowing that our customers see us in this way. And, I know I express this for the whole team, when I say “thank you” to the SAP customers who left reviews with G2 that enabled us to achieve this recognition.

Where are customers feeling the value from SAP BTP?

Of course, SAP customers use SAP BTP for a variety of business needs, and so the value they feel from the platform and services, flows to them in a number of different ways. Some of this value is described in the reviews that customers leave at the SAP BTP landing page on the G2 website. Here are a few examples:

“It effortlessly connects different systems and makes everything work together smoothly. It also gives you the tools to get creative, build what you need and adapt as your business grows…flexibility for implementation is superb and the support from SAP makes it better because of which it’s very easy and convenient.”

(User review by a mid-market customer, Dec 2024)

 

“The feature I appreciate the most in SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) is its multifunctionality and integration. It is possible to optimize business processes through a single platform which integrates several SAP and third-party systems together. Thanks to the cloud-based business services offered on the Platform, along with powerful analytics and ML capabilities, it is an excellent environment for building and releasing out-of-the-box solutions. And finally, the low-code/no-code environment allows business users themselves to create the required solutions and thus speed up digital change.”

(User review by small business customer, Sept 2024)

 

“SAP business technology is very User friendly & is an end to end solution…It basically solved all my procurement transformation needs.”

(User review by enterprise customer, Dec 2024)

So, as 2024 draws to a close, I’d like to thank both G2 for recognizing SAP BTP as a Leader in many of the 2024 reports as well as the new Winter 2025 reports, and of course thanks to our customers too, for all of the continued kind words and support, and for choosing SAP BTP as the platform to help run your business.

Thanks for a super end to 2024, and here’s to a wonderful 2025 ahead!

Chris Grundy, Product Marketing, SAP BTP

entry.title: Wrapping up 2024 for Application Development, Automation and Integration on SAP BTP: What a year!
entry.content:
2024 has been an incredibly busy year for our application development, automation and integration portfolio on the SAP Business Technology Platform. As the year draws to a close, I wanted to take some time to reflect on our achievements. Indeed, I am very grateful to the people in my teams who have worked very hard to deliver so many improvements to our solutions, and I would like to say a big thank you to all of them.

Of course, you've heard a lot about Joule, our AI co-pilot, over the course of the year. In fact, we have been integrating AI in general, and Generative AI with Joule in particular, into all components of our portfolio, as we announced earlier. And we demonstrated our AI capabilities at SAP TechEd two months ago. This deployment of Business AI has generated great interest from our customers and partners, as well as analysts.

But we've also introduced many new features and enhancements, which I'm proud to present in detail below.

SAP Build

In terms of application development and automation, in addition to embedding AI in all of our solutions, our biggest improvement in 2024 was to enrich SAP Build with pro-code capabilities. Indeed, we announced SAP Build Code at SAP TechEd 2023, made available in March this year, and we recently added ABAP Environments to SAP Build. SAP Build now allows business users and developers to work across their low-code, Java/JavaScript and ABAP projects from a unique starting point, the SAP Build lobby, benefitting of seamless interoperability capabilities fostering fusion development.

With this, SAP Build is now THE extensibility solution for any SAP customer: you can now accelerate your ERP extension development and modernization, foster innovation, and automate processes, all in a single, comprehensive suite of AI-driven tools for extension development. And SAP S/4HANA users can create extensions with SAP Build directly from the ERP business application, thanks to our new Extensibility Wizard for S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

This year, we have worked hard to improve the integration of the SAP Build components, both with other components of SAP BTP and with other SAP solutions: for example, SAP Build Process Automation is now integrated with SAP Build Apps and also with Advanced Event Mesh, applications created with SAP Build Apps can be deployed in SAP Build Work Zone, and SAP Build Work Zone supports multi-tenancy.

Finally, I would like to mention that we have improved the usability of SAP Build, for example with a new process layout and script task editor in SAP Build Process Automation and guided experiences in SAP Build Work Zone.

This image summarizes all the improvements we delivered this year:

A few weeks ago, Gartner has recognized SAP as a Visionary in its 2024 Magic Quadrant for Enterprise Low-Code Application Platforms (LCAP). I would like to thank the SAP Build team for achieving this great result!

You can find more details in these blog posts from our product experts:

  • Product Updates for SAP Build Code – November 2024
  • Product Updates for SAP Build Code – October 2024
  • What’s New in SAP Build – Q3 2024 Edition
  • Product Updates for SAP Build Code – September 2024
  • Product Updates for SAP Build Code – July 2024
  • What’s New in SAP Build – Q2 2024 Edition
  • What’s New in SAP Build – Seamless Integration of Processes and Apps
  • What’s New with SAP Build Process Automation: January 2024

SAP Integration Suite

In 2024 we have also improved SAP Integration Suite in many different areas:

Regarding SAP PI/PO migration, which is a topic on which I published a blog post earlier this year to explain Advanced Event Mesh and Edge Integration Cell, we introduced a Pipeline concept and new rules added for migration assessment.

Edge Integration Cell is now available on Google Cloud and Red Hat OpenShift. It supports outgoing connections through a HTTP proxy and provides offline availability during periods of temporary connectivity loss.

This year, Event Mesh became a capability of SAP Integration Suite, which also allows direct consumption of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Events and distributed tracing, from sending to receiving applications across one or multiple event brokers.

We have been investing heavily in improving developer efficiency and operational excellence. A few of Gen AI Increments that we recently launched were to accelerate development of integration flows with Gen AI and analyze APIs using API Anomaly detection in API Management

You can also accelerate connectivity with SAP Integration Suite using new adapters for Coupa, NetSuiteERP, SnowFlake, and Amazon DynamoDB, SMB, Jira, Amazon EventBridge, HubSpot, Anaplan with many more planned to be released soon.

We enhanced Cloud Integration and API Management user experience with SAP Horizon theme. Enriched the Cloud Integration capability with the ability to inspect content size for all the integration artifacts, manage access at the package level, validate integration flow design as per the guidelines in integration flow editor.

Furthermore, API Management now supports multiple target endpoints in API proxy tooling and recently launched Developer Hub for search, discovery and consumption of APIs. We also launched oData provisioning as a new capability.

We have onboarded 25+ partners with a contract and further enriched our marketplace with 53+ new partner content in particular 7 new APIs, 4 Processes, 20+ Integration packages and 22+ Validated partner use cases added on SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

Finally, B2B Scenarios for Trading Partner Management (TPM) and Integration Advisor have also been enriched with functionalities like archiving of Payload data for Trading Partner Management scenarios, EDI flat file payloads can be directly used to simulate mappings, for easier access to envelope fields these can be added in the Integration Advisor, simplified mass-maintenance in TPM, support for custom SOA messages in Integration Advisor, new Tradacoms message standard.

You can find more details in these blog posts from our product experts:

  • Whats New For SAP Integration Suite – August 2024
  • What’s New for SAP Integration Suite – June & July 2024
  • What’s New for SAP Integration Suite – Spring 2024
  • What’s New for SAP Integration Suite – February 2024
  • What's New for SAP Integration Suite - January 2024

Feel free to follow our #WhatsNewInSAPIntegrationSuite on SAP Community and register for our webinar for updates on upcoming innovations!

Finally, I’m also proud to share that with SAP Integration Suite SAP has been named a Leader for 4th consecutive time by Gartner® in its 2024 Magic Quadrant™ for iPaaS. In addition, SAP is a Leader in IDC MarketScape: Worldwide Business-to-Business Middleware 2024 Vendor Assessment.

Kudos to the team for these achievements!

What’s for 2025?

As you’ve seen above, we delivered a lot of new features and enhancements in 2024. And for sure we will continue in 2025!

You can find out what we have planned for next year on our public roadmap for application development, automation and integration on the SAP Business Technology Platform.

With that, I wish you a Merry Christmas and very happy holidays!  🎅🏻🎄

entry.title: MDG DQM Validation Rules – Access additional data model tables without ABAP coding
entry.content:
Background

If you want to implement medium or complex validations it’s often necessary to involve data and attribute values from tables that are not available by direct access in the rule. In the past (BAdI/coding approach) this was also called "cross entity" and it caused enhanced coding and data model knowledge. In MDG DQM this is now very easy because the framework gives you the ability to access the data by using predefined procedure calls.

This blog explains how you can access additional data model tables using these procedure calls so that this implementation can be used in all MDG processes – even to access current change request data.

Introduction

When the system calls the implementation of a validation rule, the data of the base table, the parent table of the base table, and the root table is automatically supplied.

An example for the business partner data model: If the base table is Phone (ADR2), the data from the Address (ADRC) and the General Data (BUT000) tables is automatically supplied.

An example for the product data model: If the base table is Storage Location (MARD), the data from the Plant Data (MARC) and the Basic Data (MARA) tables is automatically supplied.

But how to access data that resides in a different table of the data model?

The answer in a nutshell: Use the delivered procedure calls in BRFplus in the applications ZMDQ_147 (for business partners) and ZMDQ_194 (for products).

Steps for creating a validation rule using delivered procedure call

Let’s take an example: Create a validation rule that checks that for a business partner of category Organization either the tax number category DE0 or DE1 is assigned. This rule shall be executed in a change request.

Step 1: Create validation rule with base table General Data (BUT000)

Use the Validation Rules for Business Partners app to create a rule with some business details and base table General Data (BUT000).

Note that the base table is not Tax Numbers (DFKKBPTAXNUM) for the following reason: During execution the system loops over all entries of the rule base table – but if there is no entry (in this example: if there is no tax number at all), no error is raised.

Step 2: Add rule usage and prepare BRFplus artefacts

Add rule usage Check in Change Requests and choose Prepare.

Step 3: Implement rule scope Partner Category is equal to Organization

Navigate to the BRFplus Workbench using the link for the rule scope expression in the Implementation section. Select the field Partner Category from the context and set the value to ‘2’ (Organization).

Step 4: Implement rule condition Assigned Tax Number Category is equal to DE0 or DE1

In the following, we explain how to access data from the process table DFKKBPTAXNUM_PRC creating a table operation that uses the delivered procedure call DFKKBPTAXNUM lookup.

  • Navigate to the BRFplus Workbench using the link for the rule condition expression in the Implementation section.
  • Create a table operation:

 

 Choose Create and Navigate to Object and confirm the upcoming popup windows. 

 

Choose the operation Has at least.

To access data from table DFKKBPTAXNUM select the procedure call DFKKBPTAXNUM lookup.

 

With the next step, you need to define the selection conditions for the tax type category.

Do not forget to save your expression.

Note: Process ID, Step Number, Source System and Source ID are automatically filled during execution.

Step 5: Approve rule and enable usage

Go back to the Validation Rules app, approve the rule and enable the desired usages.

Step 6: Test rule in a change request

Using this procedure call ensures that the current data is checked for the different usages. This includes the change request as well and saves you from having to call up the change request API.

Using the Check action in a change request shows the predefined message if the data violates the rule:

Summary

These procedure calls are available for the other data model tables as well. If you cannot find them in your BRFplus application you might need to update the BRFplus infrastructure using report MDQ_UPD_INFRASTRUCTURE_VAL. If the report is not yet available in your system refer to note 3279689.

Using these procedure calls will simplify the implementation of your validation rules. As mentioned before – they are available for product master data as well.

Cheers

Sabine

entry.title: Re: /SCWM/RWEIGHT_VOLUME_UPDATE in Public Edition
entry.content:
Dear Colleague,

We have somehow located the root cause of the issue. Unfortunately the sequence of your setup is wrong and the system does not block this. The storage bin migration is done before the "Define Warehouse Attributes" (either transported or manually maintained). This causes the storage bin does not have the unit information and thus causes the issue. I think your productive environment has not been setup and please make sure the correct sequence is followed.

We are testing internally if we can use the existing report to correct this. I will update here.

Kind Regards,

Clark
