entry.title: Re: Issue with User Creation for SAP Mobile Start
entry.content:
Hi @Skylark,

this is Dennis, I'm a consultant working on topics around SAP Mobile Start. 
Please see me answer below: 

From your question it's clear that for now you are using the SAP Default Identity provider (SAP Universal ID) for all the users. This method is usually only used for Admins, as the Universal ID accounts are used to log into the SAP BTP, do the Setup and Admin work on a Global or Subaccount level. 

End-users usually don't access any services via the SAP Default Identity Provider. You would instead use the SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication (short IAS). The IAS is SAP's IdP offering for customers. You should have an own IAS tenant in which you create and manage users. This IAS tenant connects to your Subaccount and end-users will login with their IAS accounts. 

It's also possible to make use of a corporate IdP that you might already use in your company. (e.g. Microsoft Entra, Octa...). In this case you would use the IAS as a proxy to your corporate IdP and have end-users logging in via their known account that might already be in use. 

If this is a new topic for you / your company I would recommend to reach out to your SAP Contact or SAP Partner to request help on this. User Management or Security topics should be looked at in more detail to find a fitting solution for your landscape and plan ahead correctly. 

Hope that is helpful! 
Best regards, 
Dennis

entry.title: How to call RFC FM using Service Consumption Model in ABAP Cloud
entry.content:
Please follow the below step by step instructions.

  1. **Download RFC Metadata**:

   - Open transaction ACO_PROXY on your ERP ex: S4HANA-on premise system.

 

Click on Create(F8) and save the metadata file locally.

  1. **Create Service Consumption Model**:

   - In ADT, create a service consumption model.

   - Select RFC as the service type and upload the previously downloaded metadata file.

   - Follow the wizard to finalize creation.

  1. **Set Up Outbound Service and Communication Scenario**:

   - Create an outbound service with the type RFC.

 

- Create a communication scenario.

 

 

 

 

   - And in the outbound service tab, add the outbound service which is just created.

 

 

And Publish the communication scenario.

  1. **Administrator Configuration**:

   - Configure the communication system:

 

   - Set up the communication arrangement:

 

  1. **Call RFC Module in ADT Class Runner**:

   - Create a new ABAP class implementing IF_OO_ADT_CLASSRUN.

   - Copy the code from Service Consumption Model.

 

   - Save and run the class to test the RFC call.

 

Thanks,

Jaman

entry.title: Emails from User Email Id are not going

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Analytics Average Calculation
entry.content:
Hi @Abraham3 ,

Good day!!

Averaging between region will give you only single value(which is the average amount considering all the regions). So when you again add the region dimension to the chart it will any way split to individual values.

You must remove the region dimension in the chart to get the average.

Or if you want the split of regions you should be aggregating based on other dimensions.

Kind regards,

Tha.Ram

entry.title: NOW RELEASED: Upload and work with external files in SAP Cloud ALM for implementation
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM streamlines application lifecycle management for SAP customers by combining efficiency and cost-effectiveness. With its automatic updates and easy setup, it eliminates the need for hardware investments, complex configurations, or technical upgrades. Best of all, SAP Cloud ALM is free of subscription fees. Under SAP’s fair usage policy, customers benefit from built-in resources, including 8 GB of SAP HANA memory and monthly outbound API data transfers, allowing businesses to focus on growth while minimizing technical overhead.

Previously, SAP Cloud ALM users could only reference external files stored in disparate locations using URLs. Given the widespread use of Word documents, PDFs, Excel sheets, and similar formats, managing documents efficiently was a challenge.

We are excited to announce that it is now possible to upload documents directly into SAP Cloud ALM for implementation through an integration with SAP BTP Document Management Service (DMS).

About SAP BTP Document Management Service (DMS)

SAP BTP Document Management Service is a cloud-based solution for secure and scalable document storage and management. It enables businesses to seamlessly store, manage, and share files across various SAP applications and processes. This integration aligns with SAP's strategy to provide harmonized and centralized document management capabilities.

More details about SAP BTP DMS can be found here:
Document Management Service Documentation 

How to Connect SAP Cloud ALM to SAP BTP DMS

To store external files in SAP Cloud ALM, customers must request:

  1. SAP Document Management Service, Integration Option
  2. SAP Document Management Service, Repository Option

Once these services are requested, a connection between SAP Cloud ALM and SAP BTP DMS can be established, enabling seamless document uploads and management. More details on connecting the service can be found in the Set up & Admin Guide of SAP Cloud ALM.

For SAP BTP DMS there are different Service plans available.

Documents managed via SAP Cloud ALM are stored in a dedicated repository. These documents can be uploaded, replaced, or deleted exclusively through SAP Cloud ALM, ensuring proper change tracking and preparing for future enhancements, such as versioning and document history.

Core Functionalities in the First Release

The initial version introduces essential capabilities:

1. Uploading External Files

Once the integration with SAP BTP Document Management Service (DMS) is established, users can upload one file per document in SAP Cloud ALM. To do this:

  • Create a document in SAP Cloud ALM.

  • Access the "External File" tab, where you can upload a file (maximum size: 10 MB) stored on your PC or another location. Supported MIME types can be checked in the SAP Cloud ALM application help.
  • Click “Upload” to store the file in the SAP BTP DMS repository. The repository operates seamlessly in the background, invisible to end-users.

  • The file name and size will appear in the External File tab. A draft version of the document is automatically created. If the draft is discarded, the file is also deleted. To finalize, click “Create” to save the document.

Uploaded documents are fully manageable within SAP Cloud ALM: users can assign a document type, owner, status, tags, and relate the document to processes or other elements.

In the list view, documents with external files appear alongside others, and new filters enable searching specifically for these documents.

2. Replacing External Files

To update a file:

  • Download the current version via the provided link.
  • Edit locally and use the "Replace" option while in edit mode to upload the new version.

If working in draft mode, the saved version remains accessible until the changes are finalized.

3. Searching and Copying Documents

File names are searchable from the list view, even when they differ from the document title.

Additionally, documents with external files can be copied, creating a new version in the repository that can be modified independently.

4. Deleting External Files

To delete an external file:

  • Switch to edit mode and select “Delete”. Only the file in the DMS repository is removed, while the document remains.
  • To delete both the document and its external file, save changes and delete the document in the standard way. Mass deletions can also be performed from the list view.

Looking Ahead

The first release marks the beginning of capabilities in SAP Cloud ALM working with external files. Planned improvements include:

  • Document Previews
  • Versioning and Document History
  • Transfer of Documents from SAP Solution Manager

Your feedback is very welcome — please share your ideas, questions, and influence requests to help shape the future of SAP Cloud ALM.

entry.title: E2E - OIL&GAS - Integration between TD and TM
entry.content:
In a conversation with some friends about the integration between TD and TM, some questions arose regarding the E2E process, so I will briefly describe what an Outbound process from TD to TM looks like.

Below is a flow with the steps. As we can see, all the steps are already known, however, they are organized a little differently than normal. 

Two moments are important to highlight:

1 Quantity Assignment

In this step, we can follow the manual path, executing transaction O4L1 - TD-F Doc Item Quantity-Create/Change, or in a massive way, through transaction OINSC_TDQTYA - Bulk Shpmt - Mass DocItem Qty Assign. This step is the main step to start the integration between TD and TM.

2 Create Freight Unit and Freight Order

These two steps are created automatically, without the need for user intervention. It is triggered after the completion of the “Quantity Assignment” step. In a standard TM scenario, Freight Order Creation is necessary for transportation planning; in this case, the planning has already been executed by O4F1- Create Shipment.

If you notice, we do not have the VI01 – Create Shipment Costs step, this step is no longer necessary in this new design, now this is the responsibility of TM, calculating the freight cost, creating the PO and SES.

I believe that with this additional information it is possible to have a complete view of the topic.

Here are the links to the other posts.

1 Functional Information: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-for-oil-gas-and-energy-blogs/oil-amp-gas-integra%C3%A7%C3%A3o-entre-td-e-tm/ba-p/13712286

2 Technical Information: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-for-oil-gas-and-energy-blogs/technical-oil-amp-gas-integration-between-td-and-tm/ba-p/13904981

 

Help SAP

1 Integration of IS-OIL with Transportation Management: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/0f4ab800d01c4366b0c9aaff06a64320/4ceebff75b5647fda977ec70032578a5.html

2 TM-Based Shipment Costing in IS-OIL - TD: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/0f4ab800d01c4366b0c9aaff06a64320/67f7d804e9fe4a688499c2528b120776.html

entry.title: Re: Vendor Payment Process After Item Is Sold
entry.content:
Hi 

What I understood that you buy stock from vendor and make GR to own. but you made payment to vendor when you sell it.

if it is a scenario. I would suggest to use vendor consignment Process. in this when you will make gr by 101 k movement it will show stock in your company premises but you will not be liable to pay it to vendor. when you need, you bring stock in your premises you will be liable to pay to vendor and then you sell it to customer. 

see this blog it may give you idea 

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/vendor-consignment-process/ba-p/13257635

Let me know if you more information 

Regards

Rashid

entry.title: Re: Build and Run Multi-Tenant SaaS Solutions on the SAP BTP
entry.content:
Update December 2024

Business-ready artificial intelligence was the main topic at SAP in 2024. AI-driven capabilities and features paved their way into all SAP products based on the copilot Joule and the AI Foundation of the SAP Business Technology Platform. And of course partners can incorporate generative AI in their partner solutions as well.

In this spirit we added an example for a generative AI driven feature to our latest update on the Partner Reference Application. The example explains how to integrate and use the SAP AI Core service and the Generative AI Hub in our sample application "Poetry Slam Manager".

In addition to the AI-driven feature enhancements, we have adapted the ERP integration to avoid issues with the extended data validations introduced with CDS 8, and added a number of minor fixes, enhancements and tutorial updates.

A happy New Year and all the best for 2025!

entry.title: Looking for SAP Order Management solution for sourcing and availability learning material?
entry.content:
I  recommend to access our Customizing technical baseline of SAP OMF and SAP OMSA learning journey.

Overview
Welcome to the Customizing Technical Baseline of SAP Order Management Foundation and SAP Order Management Sourcing and Availability course. This comprehensive program is designed to equip you with the essential knowledge and skills for architecting and integrating SAP environments, specifically focusing on SAP Northstar Architecture, good practice principles, cloud connectivity, data ingestion, and backend setup. Throughout this course, you will learn how to effectively utilize SAP tools and methodologies to create robust and scalable solutions.

Learning objectives
After completing this course, you will be able to:

  1. Understand composable ERP and the integration of subaccounts, roles, and ID providers.
  2. Connect cloud tenants to backend systems and manage data ingestion.
  3. Implement technical integration between SAP S/4HANA ERP and data ingestion.
  4. Set up and configure iFlows, replication models, and change pointers.
  5. Monitor data processes using Cloud ALM and configure AIF settings.

Prerequisites

 

  • Exploring SAP Order Management Foundation and Sourcing & Availability

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 
I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit
